title: centaurea × beregiensis
intro: Centaurea × beregiensis, also known as the Hungarian cornflower, is a hybrid plant species within the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. Centaurea × beregiensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing individuals to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
